preview_text:
The model favors Canada at 41%, with Switzerland trailing at 28%. That 12-percentage-point gap reflects the model's assessment that Canada holds a material edge in this neutral-venue fixture. Switzerland's underdog position remains competitive but secondary in the statistical forecast.

The draw probability sits at 31%, nearly as high as Canada's win chance. This substantial draw likelihood indicates a closely matched contest where neither side is decisively favored. The three-way spread is moderately compressed, suggesting a competitive encounter without a runaway favorite. All three outcomes carry meaningful probability mass.

A meaningful divergence appears between the model and sportsbook consensus. The market backs Switzerland at 40%, inverting the model's ranking and placing Canada at 29%—roughly an 11-percentage-point swing on either side. Polymarket pricing aligns with the sportsbooks. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment is correct; both interpretations reflect legitimate analytical disagreement about the match's likely flow.
